Music and Computers

When Ticks Become Tones

If a repeating pulse happens slowly enough, we perceive each pulse as a distinct pulse. But as the repeat rate is increased they start to merge into a steady tone. The "frequency" of the tone is the number of pulses per second.

 To Do:

  • Move the "Octave" fader slowly to the right to increase the frequency.
  • Note the frequency range at which the individual pulses merge into a tone.
